assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and a terminal or editor for Python, JavaScript, or cURL.
No Exa account, team, onboarding completion, free credits, or API key exist yet.

developer_goal: Create an Exa account, obtain an API key, and execute one documented Exa API request until a search, answer, or chat completion result is returned.

first_success.outcome: An authenticated Exa Search, Answer, or Chat Completions request returns or prints its documented result.
first_success.observable_signal: Python prints the result object, JavaScript logs a result item or streamed answer, or cURL returns JSON containing the endpoint response.

route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete Dashboard-to-first-request route only after signup/onboarding fields and one language/use-case combination are fixed.

candidates.name: Search and crawl with Python SDK
candidates.reason: Documented as one of three use cases with three clients; no default is named.

candidates.name: Answer or Chat Completions with Python, JavaScript, or cURL
candidates.reason: Equal documented variants change packages, files, endpoints, and success signals.

candidates.name: API Playground-only exploration
candidates.reason: Welcome links the playground, but the QuickStart defines first success as an SDK or cURL request.

required_path.action: Create an Exa account and complete new-account onboarding required for Dashboard access and free credits.

required_fields.label: Signup and onboarding controls
required_fields.notes: Docs require an account and completed onboarding for $10 free credits but do not publish the interactive field sequence.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open the Exa Dashboard with onboarding complete.

required_path.action: Get an Exa API key from the Dashboard API Keys area.

required_fields.label: API key creation or copy controls

required_path.observable_result: A usable Exa API key value is available.

required_path.action: Store EXA_API_KEY in a .env file or export it for cURL.
required_path.observable_result: The selected client can read the API key.

required_path.action: Install the documented SDK packages for the selected language route, or skip for cURL.

required_fields.label: Package install commands
required_fields.notes: Python and JavaScript install different packages; Chat Completions also installs openai.

required_path.observable_result: Required packages are available, or cURL proceeds without installation.

required_path.action: Create the documented example for the selected use case, or prepare the cURL body.
required_path.observable_result: The request artifact for the chosen endpoint is ready.

required_path.action: Execute the selected file or cURL command and observe the returned output.
required_path.observable_result: The client prints or returns the documented Search, Answer, or Chat Completions result.

prerequisites.description: Completed Dashboard onboarding so the documented $10 free credits are available for API use.

external_gates.description: An Exa account and Dashboard access are required before an API key can be obtained.
API requests are blocked when the credit balance is exhausted until credits are added or auto recharge is enabled.

platform_outcomes.description: New accounts receive $10 in free credits when onboarding is completed.
Exa returns search, answer, or chat-completion payloads for authenticated requests.

excluded.item: Tool calling and example tutorials beyond the QuickStart
excluded.reason: Welcome links these as separate get-started cards after the first API request path.

excluded.item: Adding paid credits or configuring auto recharge
excluded.reason: Needed only after free credits are exhausted, not for the documented first request while free credits remain.

sources.title: Welcome to Exa
evidence_supported: QuickStart and API Playground entry points

sources.title: Get started with Exa
evidence_supported: Search, Answer, and Chat Completions examples

sources.title: Billing
evidence_supported: Requests blocked when balance is empty

sources.title: API Keys dashboard
evidence_supported: Authenticated API Keys management surface exists

uncertainties.question: What exact signup, verification, team, onboarding, and API-key creation controls does a new Exa developer complete?
uncertainties.impact: Unrecorded screens can add fields, waits, or approvals before free credits and the QuickStart begin.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account Dashboard walkthrough from signup through first API key copy.

uncertainties.question: Which of the nine language and use-case QuickStart combinations is the canonical shortest route?
uncertainties.impact: Installation, code, endpoint, and observable output differ by combination with no named default.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: An official recommended first request, or an explicit workshop choice of one language and one use case.
